title: [lora_l2.c:700-710] rx_callback+user_data update order could race
description: In lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback(), user_data is set before rx_callback (lines 708-709). The comment says 'ensure atomic update of callback + user_data pair' via mutex. However, if the RX thread reads the callback pointer between the two assignments (before mutex is released), it snapshots NULL callback with new user_data. This is benign since cb==NULL short-circuits the call, but the comment implies atomic semantics that aren't quite achieved. Setting callback=NULL first, then user_data, then callback=cb would ensure the old callback never sees new user_data.
close_reason: Verified: code already had correct order (user_data before callback) and mutex protection. Added clarifying comment explaining why the order matters for race safety.
